summary.en: Originally an Eli Lilly internal R&D experiment (2001), spun out as an independent company in 2005. Connects 'seekers' (companies with unsolved problems — P&G, Eli Lilly, NASA, Rockefeller Foundation, US DoD) with 'solvers' (a global network of ~500K independent scientists and amateur problem-solvers) via published challenges with cash prizes ranging from $5K to $1M. Mechanically a multi-sided platform: seekers pay listing fees + commission on awards; solvers get free access plus the prize money. Strategically the canonical *connector platform* that runs outside-in open innovation as-a-service for many seekers — a meta-application of Open Business Models. The case is dual-tagged: `multi-sided-platforms` (the structure) + `open-business-models / outside-in` (the strategy it serves).
summary.zh: 最早是礼来(Eli Lilly)2001 年的内部研发实验项目,2005 年分拆为独立公司。把「Seeker」(有未解问题的公司 —— 宝洁、礼来、NASA、洛克菲勒基金会、美国国防部)与「Solver」(全球约 50 万独立科学家和业余解题人组成的网络)通过带奖金的公开挑战赛连接起来,奖金从 5,000 美元到 100 万美元不等。机制上是一个多边平台:Seeker 付挑战发布费 + 中奖佣金,Solver 免费访问并能拿到奖金。战略层面这是把「由外而内」开放式创新**作为服务**提供给众多 Seeker 的典范连接器平台 —— 是开放式商业模式的元应用。本案例同时打了两个标签:`multi-sided-platforms`(结构)+ `open-business-models / outside-in`(它服务的战略)。

sources.label: Karim R. Lakhani et al. · 'The Value of Openness in Scientific Problem Solving' · Harvard Business School Working Paper 07-050 · 2007
Henry Chesbrough · Open Business Models: How to Thrive in the New Innovation Landscape · Harvard Business Review Press · 2006 (InnoCentive sidebar pp. 105-108)
Osterwalder & Pigneur · Business Model Generation · Wiley · 2010 (Pattern No. 5, pp. 114 InnoCentive sidebar)
Andy Zynga · 'Open Innovation Marketplaces' (Lakhani's Harvard course materials referenced)
